title: Global AI Governance Map data dictionary
caveat: This dictionary defines research data fields. It is not legal advice and should not be read as a substitute for checking official sources.
legalSeparationInvariant: Legal records drive legal map modes. Atlas, corpus, process, standards, public-sector, safety, procurement, and infrastructure records drive only context modes unless a separate verified legal record establishes legal effect.

collections.label: Institution records
collections.purpose: Track AI offices, safety institutes, standards bodies, treaty bodies, regulators, digital ministries, and other public authorities relevant to AI governance.
collections.legalEffectCaveat: Institution rows describe mandate and authority context. They do not establish a new legal duty without a separate verified legal record.

fields.name: id
fields.definition: Stable machine-readable institution ID.

fields.name: name
fields.definition: Institution name as used in the source.

fields.name: institutionType
fields.definition: Controlled taxonomy for AI office, safety institute, standards body, regulator, court, treaty body, or other institution.

fields.name: jurisdiction
fields.definition: Jurisdiction or institutional scope.

fields.name: countryIso3
fields.definition: Country or supranational geography used for map context.

fields.name: mandate
fields.definition: Source-backed description of the institution's role.

fields.name: authorityScope
fields.definition: Limit of authority, especially where a body is advisory, technical, voluntary, or not a regulator.

fields.name: powers
fields.definition: Discrete source-backed powers, functions, or activities.

fields.name: relatedRecords
fields.definition: Links to rules, instruments, obligations, processes, labs, or other corpus records.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.definition: Classifies the authority of the cited source for this claim.

fields.name: verificationStatus
fields.definition: Editorial status after source review. Uncertain records remain visible but should not drive strong legal conclusions.

fields.name: confidence
fields.definition: Confidence in the record after considering source quality, specificity, currency, and legal ambiguity.

fields.name: lastVerified
fields.definition: Date this exact source-backed claim was last checked by the editorial workflow.

fields.name: verificationNotes
fields.definition: Short source-audit note explaining what the source supports and any limits.

fields.name: reviewStatus
fields.definition: Optional editorial or expert-review state for records that need higher assurance.

collections.label: Policy-process records
collections.purpose: Track consultations, calls for evidence, hearings, standards consultations, implementation guidance, treaty negotiations, and monitoring processes.
collections.legalEffectCaveat: A process row is not a final legal interpretation or binding obligation unless the underlying legal record separately says so.

fields.name: id
fields.definition: Stable process ID.

fields.name: processType
fields.definition: Controlled taxonomy for consultation, hearing, guidance update, standards process, treaty negotiation, or monitoring process.

fields.name: stage
fields.definition: Current stage described by the source.

fields.name: status
fields.definition: Whether the process is open for action, closed, ongoing, scheduled, or monitored.

fields.name: deadline
fields.definition: Submission or action deadline where explicitly available.

fields.name: conveningBody
fields.definition: Institution convening the process.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.definition: Classifies the authority of the cited source for this claim.

fields.name: verificationStatus
fields.definition: Editorial status after source review. Uncertain records remain visible but should not drive strong legal conclusions.

fields.name: confidence
fields.definition: Confidence in the record after considering source quality, specificity, currency, and legal ambiguity.

fields.name: lastVerified
fields.definition: Date this exact source-backed claim was last checked by the editorial workflow.

fields.name: verificationNotes
fields.definition: Short source-audit note explaining what the source supports and any limits.

fields.name: reviewStatus
fields.definition: Optional editorial or expert-review state for records that need higher assurance.

collections.label: Standards and conformity records
collections.purpose: Track harmonized standards work, voluntary standards, conformity-assessment infrastructure, and technical governance guidance.
collections.legalEffectCaveat: Standards are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them or grants a legal presumption of conformity.

fields.name: id
fields.definition: Stable standards/conformity record ID.

fields.name: standardsBody
fields.definition: Standards or technical-governance body responsible for the work item.

fields.name: status
fields.definition: Published, under development, work programme, guidance, or conformity context.

fields.name: legalRole
fields.definition: How the standard or conformity item relates to law or compliance context.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.definition: Classifies the authority of the cited source for this claim.

fields.name: verificationStatus
fields.definition: Editorial status after source review. Uncertain records remain visible but should not drive strong legal conclusions.

fields.name: confidence
fields.definition: Confidence in the record after considering source quality, specificity, currency, and legal ambiguity.

fields.name: lastVerified
fields.definition: Date this exact source-backed claim was last checked by the editorial workflow.

fields.name: verificationNotes
fields.definition: Short source-audit note explaining what the source supports and any limits.

fields.name: reviewStatus
fields.definition: Optional editorial or expert-review state for records that need higher assurance.

collections.label: Public-sector AI records
collections.purpose: Track public-sector registries, AI use-case inventories, algorithmic impact-assessment systems, AI procurement guidance, public pilots, and public compute context.
collections.legalEffectCaveat: Public-sector records usually apply to public bodies or procurement practice and should not be read as general private-sector AI law.

fields.name: id
fields.definition: Stable public-sector AI record ID.

fields.name: recordType
fields.definition: Registry, AI use-case inventory, AIA system, procurement guidance, public pilot, or public compute service.

fields.name: legalEffect
fields.definition: Public-sector legal or policy role.

fields.name: coveredPublicBodies
fields.definition: Public bodies or systems covered by the record.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.definition: Classifies the authority of the cited source for this claim.

fields.name: verificationStatus
fields.definition: Editorial status after source review. Uncertain records remain visible but should not drive strong legal conclusions.

fields.name: confidence
fields.definition: Confidence in the record after considering source quality, specificity, currency, and legal ambiguity.

fields.name: lastVerified
fields.definition: Date this exact source-backed claim was last checked by the editorial workflow.

fields.name: verificationNotes
fields.definition: Short source-audit note explaining what the source supports and any limits.

fields.name: reviewStatus
fields.definition: Optional editorial or expert-review state for records that need higher assurance.

collections.label: Enforcement and litigation records
collections.purpose: Track official enforcement actions, regulator investigations, litigation, public incidents, and policy warnings relevant to AI governance.
collections.legalEffectCaveat: Enforcement records are event evidence. They do not create map binding-law coloring unless the event is itself a verified binding order or tied to a verified legal rule.

fields.name: id
fields.definition: Stable enforcement or incident record ID.

fields.name: eventType
fields.definition: Controlled event type such as enforcement action, investigation, litigation, public incident, or policy warning.

fields.name: proceduralStage
fields.definition: Current procedural stage where available.

fields.name: outcomeOrRemedy
fields.definition: Outcome, remedy, or relief where officially available.

fields.name: officialDocketUrl
fields.definition: Official docket, regulator action, or court source where available.

fields.name: sourceKind
fields.definition: Classifies the authority of the cited source for this claim.

fields.name: verificationStatus
fields.definition: Editorial status after source review. Uncertain records remain visible but should not drive strong legal conclusions.

fields.name: confidence
fields.definition: Confidence in the record after considering source quality, specificity, currency, and legal ambiguity.

fields.name: lastVerified
fields.definition: Date this exact source-backed claim was last checked by the editorial workflow.

fields.name: verificationNotes
fields.definition: Short source-audit note explaining what the source supports and any limits.

fields.name: reviewStatus
fields.definition: Optional editorial or expert-review state for records that need higher assurance.

confidenceLadder.label: Official legal text
confidenceLadder.definition: Primary statute, regulation, treaty text, gazette, official treaty-status page, or authoritative legal instrument source.

confidenceLadder.label: Official regulator or government page
confidenceLadder.definition: Government, regulator, public authority, treaty body, or intergovernmental page describing mandate, implementation, guidance, process, or enforcement status.

confidenceLadder.label: Issuer-controlled source
confidenceLadder.definition: Company, standards body, index publisher, research institute, or framework issuer page used for context, commitments, scores, or technical material.

confidenceLadder.label: Standards body source
confidenceLadder.definition: ISO, IEC, CEN, CENELEC, NIST, or similar source for standards and conformity infrastructure. Legal role depends on incorporation by a separate verified legal record.

confidenceLadder.label: Reputable secondary context
confidenceLadder.definition: Used only as discovery or clearly labeled context. It should not be the basis for binding-law coloring or strong legal-effect summaries.

confidenceLadder.label: Inferred relationship
confidenceLadder.definition: Analytical connection drawn from structured data, such as lab market-access exposure. It must include rationale, directness, caveat, and confidence metadata.

editorialRules: Do not let Atlas or corpus context indicators affect binding-law map coloring.
Do not treat signature as ratification, membership coverage as explicit endorsement, or standards as national law.
Use official or issuer-controlled sources for high-impact public claims.
If official verification is blocked, keep the record visible with a caveat and manual-review note rather than upgrading confidence.
Uncertain or low-confidence records must not drive strong legal-effect summaries.
